rationale: Both correctly diagnose the None+1 crash from missing extended ratio tokens on the base tokenizer, cite the same file/line, give the identical confirmed workaround, and correctly close with a follow-up issue per Gaohan123. X is slightly better grounded: it quotes the actual yaml headers to justify the topology claim and offers a concrete, degrade-safe code diff for the guard, whereas Y fabricates specifics not in the thread (attributing PR #2713 to 'adding AR support'/ratio-slice logic, when the thread actually blames #2713 for the yaml forcing two-stage topology unconditionally) and adds an unverified 'NPU hardware' confirmation and extra i2t/t2t config rows not backed by the ground truth. Both are otherwise comparably complete.
